items.curatedReasonEn: The WalkBack program (walking and education) for recurrent low back pain prevention is acceptable to physiotherapists, but its effective implementation relies on adequate training, program flexibility, and system-level support. When implementing similar programs in your clinic, address these barriers.
items.limitation: 本研究为定性研究，结果可能受访谈者主观感知影响，且样本量较小。
items.limitationEn: This was a qualitative study, so results may be influenced by interviewees' subjective perceptions, and the sample size was small.

items.title: Developing a patient decision aid for Femoroacetabular Impingement Syndrome: A mixed-methods study.
items.summary: This mixed-methods study developed and user-tested a patient decision aid comparing non-surgical management and hip arthroscopy for femoroacetabular impingement syndrome (FAIS), including a military-related section.
items.curatedReason: 患者决策辅助工具的开发对股骨髋臼撞击综合征 (FAIS) 的非手术管理和髋关节镜手术的选择至关重要。作为临床医生，你可以借鉴此类工具的设计理念，优化你与患者的共同决策过程。
items.titleZh: 股骨髋臼撞击综合征患者决策辅助工具的开发：一项混合方法研究。
items.summaryZh: 这项混合方法研究开发并用户测试了一项患者决策辅助工具，用于比较股骨髋臼撞击综合征 (FAIS) 的非手术管理和髋关节镜手术，并额外增加了与军事相关的部分。
items.curatedReasonEn: The development of patient decision aids is crucial for shared decision-making in femoroacetabular impingement syndrome (FAIS), particularly regarding non-surgical management versus hip arthroscopy. As a clinician, you can draw from the design principles of such tools to optimize your patient consultations.
items.limitation: 本研究为混合方法研究，主要关注决策辅助工具的开发和用户测试，而非其对临床结局的直接影响。
items.limitationEn: This was a mixed-methods study focused on the development and user-testing of a decision aid, not its direct impact on clinical outcomes.

items.title: Inter-rater reliability and construct validity of a cross-diagnostic movement quality score for rehabilitation assessment.
items.summary: This cross-sectional study (N=84) found that a Movement Quality Score (MQS) had good inter-rater reliability (ICC [2,1] = 0.98) and construct validity with FIM motor total scores (ρ = 0.77) across neurological, musculoskeletal, and internal medical conditions in hospitalized patients.
items.curatedReason: MQS 在康复评估中显示出良好的评定者间信度和结构效度，意味着它可能是评估多种诊断患者运动质量的可靠工具。你可以在你的临床实践中考虑引入该量表进行评估。
items.titleZh: 跨诊断运动质量评分在康复评估中的评定者间信度和结构效度。
items.summaryZh: 这项纳入 84 名住院患者的横断面研究发现，运动质量评分 (MQS) 在神经、肌肉骨骼和内科疾病患者中具有良好的评定者间信度 (ICC [2,1] = 0.98) 和与 FIM 运动总分的结构效度 (ρ = 0.77)。
items.curatedReasonEn: The MQS demonstrated good inter-rater reliability and construct validity in rehabilitation assessment, suggesting it could be a reliable tool for assessing movement quality across various diagnostic groups. Consider incorporating this scale into your clinical practice.
items.limitation: 本研究为横断面设计，无法评估 MQS 在患者康复过程中的敏感性或预测价值。
items.limitationEn: This was a cross-sectional study, so it could not assess the sensitivity or predictive value of MQS during patient rehabilitation.

items.title: Online group exercise for people with Parkinson's disease: rehabilitation and fitness professionals' experiences to inform physiotherapy practice and service delivery - a qualitative interview study.
items.summary: This qualitative interview study with 10 rehabilitation and fitness professionals explored their experiences delivering online group exercise for people with Parkinson's disease (PwP), highlighting current practices and challenges for informing physiotherapy practice.
items.curatedReason: 物理治疗师和健身专业人员对帕金森病患者在线团体运动的经验，对于优化远程康复服务至关重要。你在设计和实施在线运动项目时，应考虑这些实践经验，确保服务质量和可及性。
items.titleZh: 帕金森病患者在线团体运动：康复和健身专业人员的经验，以指导物理治疗实践和服务交付——一项定性访谈研究。
items.summaryZh: 这项对 10 名康复和健身专业人员进行的定性访谈研究，探讨了他们为帕金森病患者提供在线团体运动的经验，以期指导物理治疗实践和服务交付。
items.curatedReasonEn: The experiences of physiotherapists and fitness professionals in delivering online group exercise for people with Parkinson's disease are crucial for optimizing telerehabilitation services. When designing and implementing online exercise programs, you should consider these practical insights to ensure service quality and accessibility.
items.limitation: 本研究为定性研究，样本量较小，结论可能不具普遍代表性。
items.limitationEn: This was a qualitative study with a small sample size, so the findings may not be broadly representative.

items.title: Early rehabilitation in trauma patients: an observational study from two Norwegian trauma centers.
items.summary: This observational cohort study (N=317) from two Norwegian trauma centers found that only 10% of severely injured trauma patients received assessment by a physical and rehabilitation medicine specialist within 72 hours, falling short of national recommendations.
items.curatedReason: 挪威创伤中心对重伤患者的早期康复服务（尤其物理和康复医学专家的会诊）并未达到国家建议水平。这提示你，即使有指南，实际临床实践中也可能存在执行缺口，尤其在早期干预方面。
items.titleZh: 创伤患者的早期康复：一项来自两家挪威创伤中心的观察性研究。
items.summaryZh: 这项来自两家挪威创伤中心、纳入 317 名患者的观察性队列研究发现，仅有 10% 的重伤创伤患者在 72 小时内接受了物理和康复医学专家的评估，未达到国家建议水平。
items.curatedReasonEn: Early rehabilitation services for severely injured trauma patients in Norwegian trauma centers, especially consultations by physical and rehabilitation medicine specialists, fall short of national recommendations. This suggests that even with guidelines, there can be implementation gaps in clinical practice, particularly regarding early intervention.
items.limitation: 本研究为观察性队列研究，数据来自挪威创伤登记处和病历，可能存在数据收集偏倚。
items.limitationEn: This was an observational cohort study, with data collected from the Norwegian Trauma Registry and medical records, which may introduce data collection bias.
